name: Isla de Cíes – Sud trail
name_original: Isla de Cíes – sendero de Sud
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Illas Cíes are found within an archipelago off the coast of Pontevedra, Galicia, Spain, characterized by a striking contrast between rugged western cliffs and gentle, sandy eastern shores. The islands are part of the Atlantic Islands of Galicia National Park, offering diverse landscapes for outdoor activities. The terrain includes extensive forests, unique rock formations, and coastal paths that provide panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean and neighboring islands. Best family friendly hiking trails in Illas Cíes The most popular family friendly hiking route is Isla de Cíes – Sud trail, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers varied coastal scenery and views across the islands. Another top favourite among local hikers is Monteagudo Route, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This trail provides impressive Atlantic views and leads past unique rock formations on Monteagudo island. Local hikers also love the Camping Islas Cíes – San Martiño Island loop from Parque Nacional Marítimo Terrestre das Illas Atlánticas de Galicia, a 3.6 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through diverse island landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Hiking in Illas Cíes is defined by dramatic coastlines, lighthouses, and diverse ecosystems, with options ranging from easy walks to moderate routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, making it suitable for families. The routes in Illas Cíes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Illas Cíes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are there in Illas Cíes?
A: Illas Cíes offers a good selection of family-friendly hiking trails. You'll find over 50 routes suitable for families, with 27 rated as easy and another 27 as moderate, ensuring options for various ages and fitness levels.

Q: What are some easy hiking trails suitable for families with young children in Illas Cíes?
A: For families with young children, easy trails are plentiful. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40331515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferreiriños Cliffs – Peito Lighthouse loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is relatively short and offers beautiful coastal views. Another gentle option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850472" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camping Islas Cíes – San Martiño Island loop&lt;/a&gt;, providing a pleasant walk with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks and viewpoints can families discover on the trails?
A: Illas Cíes is rich in natural beauty and stunning viewpoints. Families can explore dramatic coastlines and visit iconic lighthouses like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3455232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peito Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, which offers impressive Atlantic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4930984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ferreiriños Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; provide a spectacular backdrop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3455406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro da Porta&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent views towards San Martiño Island. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/C%C3%ADes_Islands" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro de Cíes&lt;/a&gt;, located on the highest point of Faro Island, for dizzying panoramas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Illas Cíes that are good for families?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes perfect for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6951054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Causeway of O Vao – Peito Lighthouse loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, allowing you to experience diverse landscapes. These loops are ideal for ensuring you return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit Illas Cíes for family hiking?
A: The best time to visit Illas Cíes for family hiking is typically during the spring (April to June) and early autumn (September to October). During these periods, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making it comfortable for walking, and the islands are less crowded than in the peak summer months. This allows for a more relaxed and enjoyable family experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Illas Cíes?
A: The Illas Cíes hiking experience is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the pristine natural environment, the stunning panoramic ocean views, and the diverse landscapes that make each trail unique. The well-maintained paths and the serene atmosphere are also frequently highlighted.

Q: What should we bring for a family day trip hiking in Illas Cíes?
A: For a family day trip, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hats, sunscreen), and comfortable walking shoes. Given the island's protected status, there are limited facilities, so packing a picnic is a good idea. Binoculars can enhance wildlife spotting, especially for seabirds.

Q: Are there any beaches suitable for families to relax on after a hike?
A: Absolutely. After a hike, families can relax on the beautiful beaches. Rodas Beach, often cited as one of the best beaches in the world, is famous for its white sand and crystal-clear waters. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/327188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Figueiras Beach&lt;/a&gt; is another notable option, offering a tranquil spot to unwind.

Q: Is it possible to see wildlife during family walks in Illas Cíes?
A: Yes, Illas Cíes is a fantastic place for wildlife spotting. The islands are a critical habitat for various seabirds, including Spain's largest cormorant population and significant colonies of yellow-legged gulls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/327195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geological Pools on Monte do Príncipe&lt;/a&gt; and Laguna de los Nenos are excellent spots for observing marine flora and fauna.

Q: How do we get to Illas Cíes, and what about permits?
A: Access to Illas Cíes is primarily by ferry from mainland ports like Vigo, Cangas, or Baiona. As a protected area within the Atlantic Islands of Galicia National Park, visiting requires prior authorization. It is crucial to obtain a permit in advance, especially during peak season, as visitor numbers are limited to preserve the natural environment. This permit is usually obtained when booking your ferry ticket.

Q: Are there any moderate trails that offer a good challenge for older children?
A: For families with older children looking for a bit more challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991272434/isla-de-cies-sud-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla de Cíes – Sud trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate hike with rewarding views. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991264723/monteagudo-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monteagudo Route&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to Alto do Príncipe, the highest point of Monte Agudo, providing breathtaking vistas and diverse landscapes.
tips: The Cíes Lighthouse Route is the longest and most popular, and offers some of the most spectacular views of the Vigo estuary.
